MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMIR GUPTA, PRAVANA CAPITAL PARTNERS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Amir Gupta and Pravana Capital Partners, Inc.'s motion to strike is denied in its entirety. The motion largely repeats arguments from defendants' unavailing demurrer, so the court's rulings on them are incorporated by reference. Further, the complaint's fraud claims are a basis for punitive damages. (CCP 3294.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AMIR GUPTA, PRAVANA CAPITAL PARTNERS, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Amir Gupta and Pravana Capital Partners, Inc.'s demurrer is overruled in its entirety.

The complaint adequately pleads breach of contract against Gupta. (See Opp. 4:14-5:3.) Gupta improperly argues facts, when the complaint's allegations must be accepted as true on demurrer. (Memo. 9:15-17; Del E. Webb v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) Moreover, where, as here, a contract is attached to the complaint, plaintiff's interpretation of it must be accepted as correct on demurrer. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.)

The complaint adequately pleads breach of fiduciary duty against Gupta. (Id. at 14:11-15:23.) With "no relevant construction experience," plaintiff pleads that she relied on her project manager Gupta to completely control the project, spend her money judiciously and look after her best interests. (Id.) These are hallmarks of a fiduciary relationship and no authority is cited for a notion that a project manager cannot be a fiduciary. (Rickel v. Schwinn Bicycle Co. (1983) 144 Cal.App.3d 648, 654.)

The complaint adequately pleads fraud and negligent misrepresentation against Gupta and Pravana with particularity. (See, e.g. id at 6:4-8, 6:17-24, 8:12-16, 15:24-17:19, 19:16-20:14.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
